rationale: The citing paper discusses Kurt, Inman, and Argo (2011) in detail in its theory section, summarizing that friend presence activates stereotype-consistent impression management and produces different spending patterns for agentic versus communal consumers. It uses this evidence to support its core framing that gender-linked agency/communion orientations can drive voluntary payment behavior, and it also includes the paper in its summary table of key prior findings. The citation is revisited in the general discussion to interpret related payment phenomena (for example, tipping), indicating an ongoing conceptual role rather than a passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as prior empirical support that agency-communion orientation and gender are linked to spending behavior in social contexts. It uses that foundation to motivate and position its own PWYW gender-payment framework.
